author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: I would assume there is a certain number of jobs currently on the network. Each job pays. As the jobs increase the rewards increase, if the same number of ULOs are active. This then will make it more interesting to run a license, so more people do that. So now the rewards and jobs are split among more people and thus the individual rewards go down.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: BUT, the larger the network, the more interesting it becomes, which then will lead to more jobs/tasks deployed.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: So this will be a back an forth till we reached full saturation of active ULOs.

author: Dodo | MRCY Nodes | UNITY - MN - WM - CHIRP
msg: But the general direction is up

author: Quoia75
msg: For those of us who will be paying for marketing to attract ULOs to a private community, this is concerning.  If Unity wants UNOs to act as a de facto marketing team we need more tools to help our license operators find our licenses in the marketplace, and keep our licenses once a lease is up.

author: brickman1914
msg: Where?  pray tell

author: brickman1914
msg: I forced stop and restarted

author: James_WM_ENO
msg: So now licences are making $6-8 a month I guess it won’t be long until the fees will need paying

author: ⚡️BeyondLimitsNodes⚡️
msg:
Agree. At this moment, I see it like this: no one outside of us and our marketing efforts even knows about the app.
We onboard ULOs, communicate with them, guide them through the entire process… everything and more.
And then they end up finding absurd 10/90% “desperation” licenses on the market. These licenses will eventually be unbound, deleted, or killed - and the ULOs will lose their licenses as well.

Suggestion:
Do not limit the UNO/ULO percentage when a UNO creates a license.

Instead, set a minimum limit for licenses listed on the marketplace - for example, at least 40% for UNOs.
Otherwise, all marketing efforts at this stage will be undermined by the unserious behavior of individuals offering 10/90% splits.

author: G P
msg: [Photo] Is there some info on what this section does exactly? Is this for a shared NFT node for example where we would split the licenses?

author: Quoia75
msg:
I agree with the marketplace limitation (30%-40% IMO) while allowing indie node operators to set limits however they want. I’m a bit confused why the poll was started by Unity last year if what UNOs requested wasn’t going to to be implemented. Maybe the limit is in-coming?

Another approach could have been to make two flavors of the Unity App: one that is available for download from Playstore and Apple which contains the marketplace link, and another that is for white label use by independent UNOs that does not contain a link to the marketplace.

author: Quoia75
msg: Either way – I can’t seriously market my licenses if the ULOs I bring in are just going to immediately migrate somewhere else. I plan on supporting some charitable causes with some of my earnings, but definitely not like this.

author: NorthLine Nodes
msg: Yes a license lease referral code.

author: Wattdog
msg: Jeez bet you’ve got King Kong thighs 😂

author: ⚡️BeyondLimitsNodes⚡️
msg: Marketplace shouldn't allow 10% 90% leases. It's destructive. Someone called me a scammer after he found one at 10/90😂

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: How do you solve the problem of high fixed percentages for UNOs making licences unattractive to ULOs in the initial stages?

author: Calus B
msg: More people and referral program

author: Quoia75
msg:
That problem will take of itself once they realize no one will take up these licenses if better terms are available. For the marketplace having a capped high for both UNO and ULO earnings would keep the marketplace democratic, while those who are privately distributing can do what they want. 

Again, I'm confused why a poll was even posted last year🤫

If there were better tools in place to help UNOs who pay for marketing retain their ULOs I honestly wouldn't be concerned about what happens on the marketplace, but since we aren't passive investors but "franchisees", I am.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: Say a licence generates 10 USD per month. At a 50:50 split, say, it is not attractive to people to take. However, a 10:90 split may be just enough to get people interested. Assuming that revenue increases, this is a very short term hump to get over. I don't see how a fixed split helps. It will just delay mass adoption until revenue increases. Having the fixed higher split doesn't alter the point at which you start making money if you want that higher split. It just means that no one makes money until revenue can support that split level. Also, this doesn't seem to have much effect on one-to-one marketing in my experience. People are happy to get my 50/50 split offer on the understanding that I won't rug them

author: Wattdog
msg: My only thought on this is that at the moment with rewards being low,  we as UNO have to offer a higher percentage to ULO in order to help spread the licences for at least the next 3/6 months. When the rewards are higher and people know about Unity, then certainly cap it at 40% uno. I’ve currently got mine on for 80% ULO as I believe it’s the only way to spread this quickly. Then after a few months, maybe years, I’ll change it

author: Elias Nikolaidis
msg: My  understanding is that the code is valid as long as the lease doesn't change

author: Quoia75
msg:
That’s an interesting perspective and it highlights a vital point: one tactic that will be necessary to retain your ULOs is to distinguish your operations from competitors. I’m glad your folks are so far staying loyal. Whatever a UNO can do to make the onboarding process as positive as possible will help. 

However it does not guarantee that some ULOs will not migrate once things start really kicking into gear. The conversion optimization part of my brain is having a hard time accepting paying for an advertising campaign that will have to contend with unnecessary leaks via the marketplace. I may be over thinking it. Everything is theory until put into action.

At minimum, it would be great to have some **brand tickers in the marketplace** to attach to licenses so ULOs can be directed to search for a particular UNO’s licenses.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I completely agree about marketing under the current conditions. It simply wouldn't make any sense.
